LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF BECHAROFF CAPITAL CORPORATION'S APPLICATION FOR ORDER THAT ROSA CORREA, ROBERTO TONY CORREA AND KIM TRAN, SHOW CAUSE WHY THEY SHOULD NOT BE HELD IN CONTEMPT OF COURT FOR VIOLATING ASSIGNMENT ORDER ENTERED ON APRIL 27, 2017. THE CAUSE IS ARGUED AND IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR. THE COURT ORDERS THE TRANS TO DELIVER PAYMENTS TO PLAINTIFF COUNSEL, MR. BREWER. MR. BREWER SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND INCLUDES THAT THE LANDLORD IS BARRED FROM SEEKING TO EVICT OR TAKING ANY OTHER RETALITORY ACTIONS AGAINST THE TRANS. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER, CSR NO. 6576, E-MAIL: RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ROSA CORREA'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND IS CONTINUED TO FEBRUARY 21, 2018 FOR FURTHER HEARING. SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION MAY BE FILED BY 2/09/18; SUPPLEMENTAL REPLY BY 2/15/18. THE COURT ALLOWED PLAINTIFF TO CONDUCT ONE DEPOSITION ON MS. CORREA. THE DEPOSITION IS TO OCCUR BY JANUARY 31, 2018 AND SHALL BE LIMITED TO ISSUES RAISED BY THIS MOTION. ALL COLLECTION EFFORTS ARE STAYED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: G. GONZALES; REPORTER: DENISE DOUCETTE, CSR#5963, TEL. 707.246.3323. [302]

ANY MONIES RECEIVED BY BECHAROFF AS A RESULT OF ITS COLLECTION EFFORTS MUST BE PAID TO MS. CORREA THROUGH HER COUNSEL NO LATER THAN MARCH 5, 2018. ALL ORDERS REGARDING COLLECTION ON THE DEFAULT JUDGMENT ARE NOW VACATED. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER OR MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-23-2018 Off Calendar

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Feb-21-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Lance Brewer, Esq. 714-424-6300 ext. 201 of Brewer & Brewer for Plaintiff Becharoff Capital Corporation.
Thomas Lalanne, Esq. 415-434-1122 for Defendant ROSA CORREA.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues motion to set aside Default. Defendant argues only evidence. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ROSA CORREA's MOTION FOR ORDER SETTING ASIDE AND VACATING DEFAUT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT AND GRANTING DEFENDANT LEAVE TO DEFEND (CCP 473.5).

Defendant Rosa Correa's motion to set aside the default and the default judgment entered against her on July 28, 2016 is granted. Ms. Correa has shown that she is entitled to have both the default and default judgment set aside per CCP 473.5 because she had no knowledge of this case until her tenants informed her of it in or about September 2017 and she promptly moved to set aside the default and default judgment. While plaintiff Becharoff Capital Corporation has shown that it mailed numerous documents to Ms. Correa and substitute served her on two different occasions, it has no proof that Ms. Correa received any of those documents. Ms. Correa's ill health, her decision to move away from San Francisco during most of the relevant time, and her total lack of interest in mail support Ms. Correa's testimony that she was unware of this lawsuit until the fall of 2017. Ms. Correa must file an answer no later than March 5, 2018. Any monies received by Becharoff as a result of its collection efforts must be paid to Ms. Correa through her counsel no later than March 5, 2018. All orders regarding collection on the default judgment are now vacated.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)

Notice Of Motion For Order Abating Action For Failure To Qualify To Transact Intrastate Business Under Corporations Code 2105
Off Calendar Feb-21-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Lance Brewer, Esq. 714-424-6300 ext. 201 of Brewer & Brewer for Plaintiff Becharoff Capital Corporation.
Thomas Lalanne, Esq. 415-434-1122 for Defendant ROSA CORREA.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues motion to set aside Default. Defendant argues only evidence. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ROSA CORREA's Motion For Order Abating Action For Failure To Qualify To Transact Intrastate Business Under Corporations Code section 2105.

Defendant Rosa Correa's motion for an order abating this action due to plaintiff Becharoff Capital Corporation's failure to qualify to transact intrastate business is denied. The only evidence that Ms. Correa has shown that Becharoff has conducted any intrastate business is the lawsuits that Becharoff has filed in this state. California Corporations Code 191(c)(1) provides that filing lawsuits is not transacting intrastate business for purposes of the requirement that non-California corporations who transact intrastate business must obtain a certificate of qualification from the Secretary of State.

Order is signed.(see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)

Thomas LaLanne, Esq. of the Law Office of Thomas J. LaLanne, Tel. 415.434.1122, for defendant and moving party;
Lance A. Brewer, Esq. of Brewer & Brewer, Tel. 714.424.6300 ext. 201, for plaintiff and opposing party, by Court Call.

RULING: At the start of the hearing, Counsel for defendant Rosa Correa read on the record a medical document re: defendant's inability to attend the hearing. The matter was discussed. After hearing, DEFENDANT ROSA CORREA'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is CONTINUED to February 21, 2018 for further hearing. Supplemental opposition may be filed by 2/09/18; supplemental reply by 2/15/18. The Court allowed Plaintiff to conduct one deposition on Ms. Correa. The deposition is to occur by January 31, 2018 and shall be limited to issues raised by this motion. All collection efforts are stayed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR#5963, Tel. 707.246.3323.

Hearing On Order To Show Cause Why Kim Tran Should Not Be Held In Contempt Of Court For Violating Assignment Order Entered On April 27, 2017
Off Calendar Sep-28-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Alisa J. Kim of the law offices of Alisa J. Kim (510-736-6293) appears as counsel for plaintiff/judgment creditor, Becharoff Capital Corporation

Kim Cue Tran and Joseph Tran (415-333-1518) appear for themselves as third parties/real-parties-in-interest

No other appearance is made in this matter

The Court inquires and hears from all sides present as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

It is determined that third parties, Kim Tran and Joseph Tran (the Trans) are tenants of judgment debtor, Rosa Correa, aka Rosa Diaz. The Trans may have been paying rent to Rosa Correa through Tony Correa under threat of eviction. It appears, prima facie that Rosa Correa, aka Rosa Diaz, and others may be in contempt of Court and not the Trans. The Order to Show Cause re Contempt against the Trans is discharged and OFF CALENDAR without prejudice to re-filing. Counsel for the judgment creditor may seek an order to show cause re contempt against the judgment debtor and/or file an appropriate motion accordingly.

Judge: Charlene P. Kiesselbach; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Gena Eales, CSR No. 9176, e-mail: geales@sftc.org. (L&M302)
